message: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - touching outlines/prompts.py (adding filters param to prompt decorator, create_jinja_env helper). Will merge with your work before submitting.
agent2 here: implementing Global Filter Registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will touch outlines/prompts.py only.

agent2 update: added module-level _FILTER_REGISTRY dict + register_filter(name,func)/unregister_filter(name) funcs at top of outlines/prompts.py (right after imports). In both _template_from_str and _template_from_file, added 'env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY)' right after the builtin env.filters[...] assignments (in _template_from_str) / right after env creation (in _template_from_file), BEFORE env.from_string/get_template is called. If your local 'filters' param is applied via env.filters.update(local_filters), just make sure it happens AFTER my _FILTER_REGISTRY line so local filters win on name collisions. Should merge cleanly since we're adding to different regions mostly. Let me know if you structured _template_from_str differently (e.g. extracted into create_jinja_env helper) so we can reconcile.

agent1: I've implemented create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) that builds the env and registers builtin + custom filters (env.filters.update(filters)). Also added filters= param to prompt decorator (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) usage) and to Prompt.from_str/from_file. If your global registry also patches create_jinja_env, let's merge carefully - happy to have your registry's filters applied inside create_jinja_env alongside the passed-in filters dict. Will merge team/agent2 into my branch before submitting.

Merged your team/agent1 branch into agent2. Resolved by having create_jinja_env() apply _FILTER_REGISTRY before the local 'filters' dict, so local filters win on name collisions (verified with a quick test). Pushed to team/agent2. All 13 tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass on the merged tree.

agent1: confirmed, I extracted create_jinja_env(loader, filters) as a shared helper and merged your registry the same way you did (registry applied first, then local filters override). Merged team/agent2 into agent1, all 13 tests pass, patch.txt regenerated from team/main..HEAD. Good to submit.
